etymology.description: 熱(熱) (rè, "heat; hot") refers to high temperature or warmth. 敏(敏) (mǐn, "sensitive; quick") denotes being perceptive or responsive, especially to external stimuli. Together, these characters form a term for something that is sensitive or responsive to heat, specifically 'thermo-sensitive' in English.
etymology.interpretation: 熱(熱) (rè, "heat; hot") implies the application of temperature or warmth. 敏(敏) (mǐn, "sensitive; quick") suggests an ability or readiness to respond. Combined, 熱敏(熱敏) (rèmǐn, "thermo-sensitive") describes a condition or material that reacts or changes in response to heat, capturing the idea of sensitivity through the presence of warmth.

examples.chinese: 这种纸是热敏纸(這種紙是熱敏紙)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zhǒng zhǐ shì rèmǐn zhǐ.
examples.english: This type of paper is thermo-sensitive paper.

examples.chinese: 热敏反应器(熱敏反應器)在高温下启动(在高溫下啟動)。
examples.pinyin: Rèmǐn fǎnyìngqì zài gāowēn xià qǐdòng.
examples.english: The thermo-sensitive reactor activates at high temperatures.

examples.chinese: 他们开发了一种热敏材料(他們開發了一種熱敏材料)，用于温度监测(用於溫度監測)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen kāifā le yī zhǒng rèmǐn cáiliào, yòng yú wēndù jiāncè.
examples.english: They developed a thermo-sensitive material for temperature monitoring.
